title: Apply Now: ESRC LISS Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in the UK (Top Universities, Funded Research Opportunity)
summary: Applications are now open for the ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 under the London Interdisciplinary Social Science Doctoral Training Partnership (LISS DTP). This prestigious programme offers early-career researchers the opportunity to strengthen their academic profile, expand professional networks, and further develop research outputs at leading UK institutions including King’s College London , Queen Mary University of London , and Imperial College London . Designed for researchers in the immediate postdoctoral stage, this fellowship focuses on career development rather than funding new research projects. If you are looking to consolidate your PhD and advance your academic career in the social sciences, this opportunity is highly competitive and rewarding. About the LISS ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship The LISS DTP is offering five ESRC-funded Postdoctoral Fellowships for the 2026 intake. These fellowships aim to support scholars in enhancing their research portfolios through publications, training, mentorship, and engagement activities. Rather than funding new research, the programme emphasizes: Strengthening existing PhD research outputs Building academic and non-academic networks Developing professional and transferable skills Fellows will be hosted at one of the partner institutions and will benefit from a vibrant interdisciplinary research environment. Fellowship Benefits and Funding The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship provides substantial support, including: Funding for up to 9 months full-time or 18 months part-time Coverage of the fellow’s salary Up to £7,500 for additional costs , including: Mentorship support Travel and conference participation Training and skills development Public engagement and impact activities All full-time fellowships must be completed by 30 June 2027 . Mentorship and Collaboration Opportunities Each fellow is required to work with a mentor based at their host institution. Mentors should: Have expertise in the applicant’s research area Not typically be the applicant’s PhD supervisor Applicants are encouraged to proactively identify and contact potential mentors before applying. Additionally, collaboration with non-academic partners is strongly supported, offering valuable real-world engagement and impact. Research Focus and Interdisciplinary Scope Applications are welcome from: Single-discipline research areas Interdisciplinary projects However, social sciences must account for at least 50% of the fellowship activities, and proposals must align with one of the LISS DTP’s designated research areas. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the eligibility requirements set by the Economic and Social Research Council . Key criteria include: A completed PhD from a UK research organisation PhD awarded or viva passed (with minor amendments) by 1 June 2026 , and fully awarded by 1 October 2026 No more than 15 months of active postdoctoral experience by 1 June 2026 Only one application to a single Doctoral Training Partnership (DTP) Applicants needing a UK work visa must ensure eligibility Notably, prior ESRC funding is not required to apply. Application Process The application process consists of two stages : Stage 1: Expression of Interest (EOI) Applicants must: Review the ESRC call specifications and eligibility criteria Identify and contact a potential mentor Submit an online EOI application Applicants should also complete the online Diversity Monitoring Form Deadline: 13 April 2026 at 10:00 AM Applicants must also complete a Diversity Monitoring Form. Only shortlisted candidates will proceed to the next stage. Stage 2: Full Proposal Shortlisted applicants will be invited to submit a full proposal. Deadline: 1 June 2026 at 23:59 Competition Timeline EOI Deadline: 13 April 2026 EOI Results: End of April 2026 Full Proposal Deadline: 1 June 2026 Interviews: Early July 2026 Final Decisions: July 2026 Submission to ESRC: By 23 July 2026 Fellowship Start Date: 1 October 2026 Fellowship End Date: 30 June 2027 Applicant Briefing Session Prospective applicants can attend a dedicated briefing session to learn more about the fellowship and application process. Date: 27 March 2026 Time: 1:00 PM – 2:00 PM Click Here , for more details on the felowship. This session is highly recommended for applicants seeking clarity and guidance before submitting their application. The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship at LISS DTP is a valuable opportunity for early-career researchers aiming to build a strong academic foundation in the social sciences. With structured mentorship, financial support, and access to leading UK institutions, fellows are well-positioned to advance their careers and make meaningful contributions to their fields. Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early—especially in identifying mentors and refining their research development plans—to maximize their chances of success. Discover other similar opportunities here. The post Apply Now: ESRC LISS Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in the UK (Top Universities, Funded Research Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Top Global STEM Opportunity 2026: Nature Awards for Inspiring Women in Science
summary: Are you passionate about advancing gender equality in STEM? Do you actively encourage girls and young women to explore science, technology, engineering, and mathematics? The Nature Awards for Inspiring Women in Science 2026 is now open for applications—offering a powerful platform to recognize, support, and amplify the work of women driving change in science across the globe. This prestigious international award celebrates both scientific excellence and impactful outreach efforts that inspire the next generation of female scientists. If you are making a difference in STEM or know someone who is, this opportunity could elevate your work to a global stage. About the Nature Awards for Inspiring Women in Science The Nature Awards aim to honor outstanding women scientists as well as individuals and initiatives that promote STEM education and career pathways for girls and young women. The award highlights role models who are not only excelling in their fields but are also actively working to close the gender gap in science. Whether through research, mentorship, advocacy, or community engagement, applicants are evaluated on their contributions to advancing gender equality and inspiring future generations. Why You Should Apply The Nature Awards for Inspiring Women in Science is more than just a financial award—it is a career-defining opportunity. By applying, you position yourself among a global network of changemakers working to reshape the future of science through inclusivity and innovation. From international exposure to mentorship and networking, this award can significantly boost your visibility, impact, and professional growth. Award Benefits Winners and finalists of the Nature Awards for Inspiring Women in Science 2026 will receive a wide range of prestigious benefits, including financial support, global recognition, and career development opportunities. Financial Two winners will each receive US$50,000 Two runners-up will each receive US$5,000 Fully Funded Award Ceremony Winners and runners-up will be invited to attend an exclusive award ceremony in either London or New York All travel and accommodation expenses are fully covered Global Recognition Featured coverage on Nature.com , one of the world’s leading scientific platforms Recognition among global leaders in science and innovation Mentorship Opportunities Access to mentoring programs through The Estée Lauder Companies Career guidance and professional development support Networking Coaching Shortlisted applicants will be showcased on the Nature Awards website Exclusive access to a coaching and networking community of peers, experts, and industry leaders Who Should Apply? This award is ideal for: Women working in scientific research across any STEM discipline Individuals or organizations running initiatives that support girls and young women in STEM Advocates and educators promoting gender equality in science If your work contributes to breaking barriers and inspiring future female scientists, you are strongly encouraged to apply. Click Here to apply. Key Dates Mark your calendar to ensure you don’t miss any important deadlines: 28 January 2026 – Applications Open 8 April 2026 – Applications Close April to August 2026 – Application Assessment Period September 2026 – Successful Applicants Notified October 2026 – Winners Announced If you are committed to empowering women in STEM and inspiring the next generation of scientists, this is your chance to gain the recognition and support your work deserves. With generous funding, global exposure, and unparalleled networking opportunities, the Nature Awards for Inspiring Women in Science 2026 stands out as one of the most impactful opportunities in the global STEM community. Be sure to review the official guidelines, FAQs, and terms and conditions before submitting your application—and don’t wait until the last minute to apply. Discover similar opportunities here. title: Strategic Foresight Internship in Paris 2026: Join GMF’s Policy & Research Team
summary: Applications are now open for the Strategic Foresight Internship at the German Marshall Fund of the United States (GMF) . This is an exciting opportunity for aspiring policy professionals to gain hands-on experience in research, strategy, and international affairs while working in Paris , France . About the Internship The Strategic Foresight program at GMF is recruiting two interns to join its Paris office for a 6-month placement . Interns will work closely with experts and contribute to forward-looking research and policy analysis focused on global trends, international relations, and strategic decision-making. Internship Duration Start Date: August 31, 2026 End Date: February 5, 2027 This is a full-time, in-person internship based in Paris. What You’ll Gain This internship offers valuable exposure to policy research and international strategy. Key benefits include: Hands-on experience in strategic foresight and policy research Opportunity to work with a globally recognized think tank Exposure to international policy discussions and analysis Professional development in research, writing, and communication Networking opportunities within global policy circles Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for students or recent graduates interested in international relations, public policy, or strategic analysis. Applicants should: Be proficient in both French and English Have an active internship agreement (required) Demonstrate strong interest in policy, research, or global affairs Possess analytical and communication skills Why This Opportunity Matters Strategic foresight is critical in today’s rapidly changing world. This internship allows you to contribute to research that informs decision-making at high levels, helping organizations and governments prepare for future challenges. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply directly through GMF’s official website. Visit OFY here, to view more internship opportunities. title: UNIDO Junior Professional Officer (JPO) 2026 in Vienna, Austria
summary: If you are looking to launch or accelerate your career in international development, sustainability, and global policy, the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) position at United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) presents one of the most prestigious and career-defining opportunities available in 2026. What is the UNIDO JPO Programme? The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme is a highly competitive global initiative designed to groom young professionals for leadership roles within the United Nations system. Under this programme, candidates gain: Hands-on experience in international development projects Exposure to global policy frameworks such as the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Professional mentoring and structured career development The programme is government-sponsored, meaning participating countries fund their nationals to work within UN agencies like UNIDO. This specific position is part of UNIDO’s mission to promote: Inclusive and sustainable industrial development Environmental sustainability Poverty reduction through industrialization Important Eligibility Restriction (CRITICAL) This particular JPO position comes with a strict nationality requirement: Only nationals of Finland are eligible to apply This is because JPO roles are funded by donor governments, and each position is typically restricted to citizens of the sponsoring country. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates Breakdown) Eligible Category of Applicants Young professionals in international development Early to mid-career professionals seeking UN careers Individuals with technical or policy expertise in sustainability, economics, engineering, or environmental fields Eligible Country Finland only Global Insight While this specific role is limited to Finland, the broader JPO programme includes opportunities funded by countries like: Germany Japan Italy China Academic and Professional Requirements To qualify for this prestigious UN role, applicants must meet strict academic and experience standards: Education Master’s degree (or equivalent) in a relevant field such as: Economics Environmental Management Engineering International Development Work Experience Minimum 3 years of professional experience (for L2 level) Language Requirements Fluency in English (mandatory) Additional UN languages (French, Spanish, Arabic, etc.) are an advantage Key Responsibilities of the JPO Role As a Junior Professional Officer at UNIDO, you will contribute to high-impact global projects, including: Supporting sustainable industrial development initiatives Working on environmental protection and circular economy strategies Assisting governments in implementing international environmental agreements Conducting research, policy analysis, and technical advisory work In this specific role, the focus includes: Reducing hazardous chemicals in industrial supply chains Promoting circular economy models Supporting compliance with global environmental conventions Salary, Benefits, and Career Advantages Financial Benefits Competitive tax-free salary (~$85,246 annually) International UN employment benefits package Career Benefits Direct entry into the UN system Exposure to global development policy and strategy Networking with international experts and governments Strong pathway to long-term UN or international careers Professional Growth Structured mentorship Multicultural working environment Opportunity to work on global sustainability challenges Duration of the Programme Typical duration: 24 months (2 years) Possible extension up to 36 months depending on funding Important note: The position does not guarantee contract renewal Why This Opportunity is Highly Competitive This JPO role stands out because: It is hosted in Vienna, one of the UN’s major global hubs It offers direct experience with SDG implementation (especially SDG 9: Industry, Innovation, Infrastructure) It provides a rare entry point into international organizations Additionally: Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply Applications from persons with disabilities are welcomed and supported How to Apply To apply for this opportunity: Visit the official UNIDO careers portal Search for “Junior Professional Officer – Vienna ” Submit your application before the deadline Ensure your application includes: Updated CV Academic transcripts Proof of nationality (Finland) Relevant professional experience Apply here Strategic Tips to Strengthen Your Application To stand out among top global candidates: Highlight experience in sustainable development or environmental policy Demonstrate familiarity with international frameworks (SDGs, climate agreements) Showcase cross-cultural or international work experience Emphasize analytical, research, and policy skills Deadline 8th April 2026 The UNIDO Junior Professional Officer (JPO) 2026 position is more than just a job—it is a gateway into the world of international development, diplomacy, and global impact. While this specific role is limited to Finnish nationals, the broader JPO programme remains one of the most powerful entry routes into the United Nations system for young professionals worldwide. title: Her Sports Moves Mentorship Program: The #1 Career Accelerator for Women Entering the Sports Industry
summary: Introduction: Empowering Women to Break Into the Sports Industry The global sports industry continues to grow rapidly, yet women remain underrepresented in leadership, management, and decision-making roles. The Her Sports Moves Mentorship Program is designed to change that narrative by equipping women with the tools, confidence, and strategy needed to successfully enter and thrive in the sports sector. Positioned as a career accelerator , this mentorship program goes beyond traditional guidance. It offers a structured pathway for women to transition into meaningful roles across various areas of the sports industry, from management and marketing to operations and media. What is the Her Sports Moves Mentorship Program? The Her Sports Moves Mentorship Program is a comprehensive initiative focused on: Career clarity and direction Personal branding and positioning Strategic career transitions Hands-on mentorship and support It is specifically tailored for women who are: Trying to break into the sports industry Looking to pivot their careers into sports Seeking advancement within the field The program provides a holistic approach , combining professional development with personal empowerment. Why This Mentorship Program Stands Out Unlike generic mentorship initiatives, this program is built as a targeted career accelerator , offering: 1. Career Strategy Development Participants gain clarity on: Their career goals The pathways available within the sports industry How to align their skills with industry demands 2. Personal Branding Support In today’s competitive job market, visibility matters. The program helps mentees: Build a strong personal brand Optimize their professional profiles Position themselves effectively to employers 3. Confidence Building Breaking into a competitive industry requires more than skills. This program focuses on: Building self-confidence Overcoming imposter syndrome Developing a growth mindset 4. Practical, Hands-On Guidance Participants receive: Actionable career advice Real-world insights Step-by-step support throughout their journey What You Will Gain By joining the Her Sports Moves Mentorship Program, mentees can expect: A clear and actionable career roadmap Enhanced professional confidence Improved personal branding and visibility Access to mentorship and guidance Increased chances of securing roles in the sports industry The program is designed to help participants not only enter the industry but also stand out and succeed . Career Outcomes and Opportunities The program highlights successful outcomes through the achievements of its mentees, who have secured roles across different areas of the sports ecosystem. Potential career paths include: Sports management Marketing and communications Event management Media and broadcasting Sponsorship and partnerships This demonstrates the program’s ability to translate mentorship into real career results . Who is This Program For? The Her Sports Moves Mentorship Program is ideal for: Women passionate about sports and career growth Students and graduates exploring opportunities in sports Professionals looking to transition into the sports industry Women aiming to elevate their current roles in sports Whether you are at the beginning of your journey or seeking to level up , this program offers tailored support to meet your needs. Program Approach The mentorship is built around three core pillars: 1. Strategy Helping participants define their goals and create a clear plan. 2. Structure Providing a guided framework to follow step-by-step. 3. Support Offering mentorship, encouragement, and accountability. This combination ensures that mentees are not just learning—but actively progressing toward their goals. Why the Sports Industry Needs Programs Like This The sports industry is evolving, and diversity is becoming a key driver of innovation and success. Programs like Her Sports Moves: Promote gender inclusion Create pathways for women Strengthen leadership diversity Contribute to a more equitable industry By empowering women, this program plays a role in shaping the future of sports globally. Deadline No specific deadline is mentioned. Applications are likely ongoing or cohort-based, so early application is recommended. title: Peace Corps Uganda Volunteer Opportunities 2026: Community Health, Economic Empowerment & Education Roles (Fully Funded Service Program)
summary: Are you passionate about making a real, lasting impact in communities while gaining global experience? The Peace Corps Uganda Volunteer Program 2026 offers three powerful service opportunities for individuals committed to public health, education, and economic development. These fully supported international service roles— Community Health Promoter , Economic Empowerment Advisor , and School Enrichment Facilitator —provide hands-on experience, cultural immersion, and long-term career benefits while working directly with communities in Uganda. All three roles share a common mission: empowering communities through sustainable development, education, and capacity-building initiatives. Overview of Peace Corps Uganda Volunteer Program The Peace Corps is a globally recognized U.S. government initiative focused on promoting world peace, friendship, and sustainable development. Volunteers serve in communities around the world, working alongside local partners to address pressing challenges. Key Program Details (All Roles) Location: Uganda, Africa Application Deadline: April 15, 2026 Departure Date: November 4, 2026 Duration: 2 years + 3 months training Decision Notification: June 1, 2026 Shared Benefits Across All Positions All selected volunteers will receive a comprehensive benefits package, including: Financial Logistical Support Travel to and from Uganda Monthly living allowance Housing provided Medical and dental coverage Professional Development Extensive language, technical, and intercultural training Hands-on international work experience Competitive advantage for federal jobs Post-Service Benefits Approximately $10,800 service award upon completion Access to graduate school benefits and career pathways 1. Community Health Promoter (Uganda) This role focuses on improving health outcomes through community education and prevention strategies. What You Will Do Promote maternal and child health Lead awareness campaigns on disease prevention (malaria, HIV, nutrition) Train community members on hygiene and sanitation Support health data tracking and behavior change initiatives Organize community health programs and events Uganda faces a significant burden of preventable diseases, with approximately 75% of illnesses linked to preventable causes. Volunteers play a critical role in shifting focus from treatment to prevention. Who Should Apply Degree in any field OR 5 years of professional experience Interest in public health or community development Experience in health education is an advantage Learn more and apply HERE. 2. Economic Empowerment Advisor (Uganda) This role is designed to support entrepreneurship and financial independence among community members. What You Will Do Train communities in financial literacy and entrepreneurship Support microfinance and savings initiatives Develop income-generating activities (e.g., agriculture, small businesses) Facilitate business and marketing training sessions Promote sustainable economic practices Uganda has a very young population with high unemployment rates, making economic empowerment initiatives critical for long-term development. Who Should Apply Degree in business or related field OR 5 years’ experience Skills in entrepreneurship, finance, or training Interest in economic development and youth empowerment Learn more and apply HERE. 3. School Enrichment Facilitator (Uganda) This role focuses on improving education outcomes and holistic student development. What You Will Do Support English and literacy development Introduce extracurricular programs (arts, sports, clubs) Promote hygiene, nutrition, and mental wellness in schools Engage parents and communities in education Organize workshops for teachers and students With a large youth population, Uganda prioritizes education as a key driver of development, making this role essential for community transformation. Who Should Apply Degree in any field OR 5 years’ experience Interest in education, youth development, or community engagement Experience working with children is a plus Learn more and apply HERE. Training and Living Experience All volunteers undergo 10 weeks of intensive training , including: Language acquisition (e.g., Luganda, Ateso, Lango, Lusoga) Cultural immersion Technical skill development Volunteers then transition into rural or peri-urban communities , living with host families or within local communities, ensuring a deeply immersive and authentic experience. Application Process Applicants must complete the Peace Corps application process, which includes: Online application submission Interviews and selection Medical and legal clearance Pre-departure preparation The entire process typically takes approximately 9 months . Why You Should Apply Joining the Peace Corps Uganda program is more than just volunteering—it is a transformative journey. Top Reasons to Apply Make a tangible impact in underserved communities Gain international work experience Develop leadership and cross-cultural skills Build a strong global professional network Enhance your career prospects in public service, development, and global health Click HERE to discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY. The post Peace Corps Uganda Volunteer Opportunities 2026: Community Health, Economic Empowerment Education Roles (Fully Funded Service Program) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Commonwealth Distance Learning Scholarships 2026 at University of Edinburgh: Fully Funded Online Study for Global Impact
summary: Introduction: Study a UK Degree from Anywhere in the World Access to quality education remains a major global challenge, especially for students in low- and middle-income countries. The Commonwealth Distance Learning Scholarships 2026 , funded by the Commonwealth Scholarships Commission , offer a transformative opportunity to pursue a UK postgraduate degree remotely , without the need to relocate. Hosted by leading institutions such as the University of Edinburgh , this programme is designed to empower talented individuals to gain advanced knowledge and skills while contributing to sustainable development in their home countries. About the Commonwealth Distance Learning Scholarships The Commonwealth Distance Learning Scholarships are part of the UK government’s international development strategy, supported by the Foreign, Commonwealth Development Office . These scholarships aim to: Expand access to high-quality UK education Support students who cannot study abroad due to financial or personal constraints Strengthen capacity and leadership in developing countries Unlike traditional scholarships, this programme allows students to study part-time through distance learning , making it flexible and accessible. Why Choose This Scholarship? This scholarship is particularly valuable because it combines: 1. Fully Funded Education Recipients typically receive: Full tuition fee coverage Access to online learning platforms and resources Academic and technical support 2. Flexible Learning Format Study from your home country Balance education with work or personal commitments No relocation or visa required 3. Global Recognition Earn a degree from top UK universities Gain credentials respected worldwide 4. Development Impact Focus on fields that contribute to sustainable development goals Empower scholars to create impact in their communities Eligible Programmes The scholarship applies to selected postgraduate (Master’s-level) distance learning programmes offered by UK universities, including those at the University of Edinburgh . These programmes often focus on: Public health Sustainable development Education Technology and data science Environmental studies Governance and policy Applicants must ensure their chosen programme is eligible under the scholarship scheme. Who Should Apply? This scholarship is ideal for: Students who want a UK degree without relocating Professionals seeking to upskill while working Individuals committed to contributing to development in their home countries It is especially suited for candidates who demonstrate: Academic excellence Leadership potential Commitment to social and economic development Application Process Step 1: Choose an Eligible Programme Select a postgraduate distance learning course offered by a participating UK university. Step 2: Apply to the University Submit your application for admission to the chosen programme. Step 3: Apply for the Scholarship Complete the scholarship application through the official Commonwealth Scholarships platform. Step 4: Selection Process Applications are assessed based on: Academic merit Development impact potential Alignment with scholarship objectives Selection Criteria The Commonwealth Scholarships Commission evaluates candidates based on: Academic qualifications and achievements Relevance of chosen programme to development goals Potential to contribute to home country development Leadership and commitment to community impact Benefits Beyond Funding Beyond financial support, scholars benefit from: Access to global academic networks Opportunities for collaboration with international peers Enhanced career prospects Long-term professional development Deadline 📅 Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply well before the deadline to ensure all documents and requirements are completed on time. Why This Opportunity Matters The Commonwealth Distance Learning Scholarships are more than just financial support—they are a pathway to global education, leadership, and impact . By removing barriers such as relocation and cost, this programme enables talented individuals to access world-class education and contribute meaningfully to their societies. title: Apply for NASA Dragonfly Program 2026: Fully Funded Graduate Opportunity to Explore Titan
summary: The dream of exploring another world is no longer confined to science fiction. Through the Dragonfly Student Early Career Investigator Program, graduate students now have a rare opportunity to contribute directly to one of the most ambitious planetary missions ever conceived. Led by NASA and developed by the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory , the Dragonfly mission is set to explore Titan —a mysterious and Earth-like world unlike any other in our solar system. This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the program, including the mission, opportunities available, eligibility requirements, application process, and detailed project tracks. Understanding the Dragonfly Mission Dragonfly is not just another space mission—it is a revolutionary leap in planetary exploration. The mission will send a rotorcraft lander (an octocopter drone) to Titan, Saturn’s largest moon, marking the first time such a vehicle will be used on another planetary body. Titan is scientifically fascinating for several reasons: It is the only moon with a dense atmosphere It features a methane-based weather cycle , including clouds, rain, rivers, lakes, and seas Its surface contains complex organic molecules , similar to those that existed on early Earth before life began Beneath its icy crust lies a subsurface ocean of liquid water Dragonfly is scheduled to launch in 2028 and arrive at Titan in 2034 . During its 3.3-year mission, it will travel over 80 kilometers, hopping across the surface every Titan day (approximately 16 Earth days), making it the most mobile robotic lander ever deployed. About the Student Early Career Investigator Program The Dragonfly program is designed to bring fresh perspectives into planetary science by engaging graduate students—especially those without prior experience in space missions. What Participants Will Do Selected students will: Conduct research related to Titan Contribute to mission science and operational planning Assist in developing instruments and hardware Participate in testing and simulations Each student collaborates with Dragonfly mission experts and gains hands-on experience in cutting-edge space exploration. Program Structure and Benefits The program selects 2–4 graduate students annually for a two-year term . Key Features Students dedicate 30% of their time (primarily during summer and academic breaks) Work is conducted at APL and/or the mentor’s institution Hybrid format (in-person + remote work) Benefits Include Funding for travel to Dragonfly team meetings Support to publish and present research at scientific conferences Collaboration with leading scientists and engineers Exposure to real-world planetary mission operations Faculty mentors receive travel support to attend kickoff meetings Eligibility Criteria The program aims to broaden participation, especially among students without prior exposure to planetary science or spacecraft missions. Basic Requirements Applicants must: Be U.S. citizens Be enrolled in a Master’s or PhD program in: Physical sciences Biological sciences Computer science Mathematics Engineering Have a minimum GPA of 3.0 Demonstrate ability to conduct independent research Possess strong communication and organizational skills Identify a faculty mentor willing to support them throughout the program Students without backgrounds in planetary science, geosciences, or atmospheric science are especially encouraged to apply . Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Cover letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) Faculty advisor’s name and contact details A 2–3 sentence statement from the faculty advisor confirming support Important Dates Application Deadline: May 29, 2026 Selection Announcement: September 30, 2026 If you experience submission issues, documents can be emailed directly to the program contact. Specialized Project Opportunities In addition to the main program, Dragonfly offers Student Guest Investigator roles focused on specific technical and scientific challenges. These projects are also two-year hybrid commitments. 1. Modeling Impacts on Titan Using Numerical Simulations This project focuses on understanding how impact craters form on Titan using high-fidelity simulations. What You’ll Do Model heliocentric and planetocentric impact events Analyze crater formation, melt volume, and surface changes Develop scripts using Python or MATLAB Work with large datasets and simulation outputs Ideal Candidates Strong analytical thinkers Experience in coding and modeling Interest in planetary surface processes 2. Prototype Development for Spacecraft Integration This hands-on engineering project involves designing a machine to fabricate non-flight cables used in spacecraft testing. What You’ll Do Study existing harness fabrication processes Design and build a prototype system Conduct testing and validation Collaborate with engineers and technicians Ideal Candidates Background in mechanical, electrical, or software engineering Experience with prototyping or automation systems Strong problem-solving skills 3. Simulating Seismic Scattering on Titan This project explores how seismic waves travel through Titan’s icy crust using computational modeling. What You’ll Do Run simulations using advanced modeling tools Analyze seismic wave propagation Compare Titan data with Earth, Mars, and Moon models Use high-performance computing systems Ideal Candidates Background in physics, mathematics, or engineering Experience with coding and simulations Knowledge of calculus and wave mechanics Faculty Mentor Requirement All applicants must secure a faculty mentor who agrees to: Provide workspace and institutional support Participate in the Fall 2026 kickoff meeting Acknowledge that the project is supplementary , not a replacement for thesis research Why This Opportunity Matters The Dragonfly Student Early Career Investigator Program is more than an academic experience—it is a gateway into the future of space exploration. Participants contribute directly to a mission that could reshape our understanding of how life begins in the universe. Working on Titan offers a unique perspective on Earth’s early chemistry, making this mission one of the most important astrobiology investigations of our time. If you are a graduate student eager to apply your STEM skills to real-world challenges and contribute to a historic mission beyond Earth, this program offers an unmatched opportunity. Whether your interests lie in simulation, engineering, or scientific analysis, Dragonfly provides a platform to turn curiosity into discovery. This is your chance to be part of a mission that will explore one of the most Earth-like yet alien environments in our solar system—and potentially answer one of humanity’s biggest questions: how life begins. Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Apply for NASA Dragonfly Program 2026: Fully Funded Graduate Opportunity to Explore Titan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Back to Inspire: Mastercard Foundation Ghana Alumni Mentorship Initiative
summary: The Back to Inspire: From Here to There initiative is a national mentorship program designed to connect alumni with students in Senior High Schools (SHS) and Senior High Technical Schools (SHTS) across Ghana. Led by the Mastercard Foundation Alumni Network – Ghana Chapter, in collaboration with the Ghana Education Service (GES) and T-TEL, this initiative aims to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world opportunities. Unlike traditional opportunities such as scholarships or internships, this program focuses on mentorship and knowledge-sharing , giving alumni the chance to directly influence the next generation by sharing their lived experiences, career journeys, and lessons learned. About the Opportunity The Back to Inspire initiative is built on the belief that alumni are powerful agents of change. Having successfully navigated the education system and transitioned into careers or entrepreneurship, alumni are uniquely positioned to guide current students. Through this program, selected alumni will return to their former schools—or support schools within their regions—to engage with students through mentorship activities. These engagements may include career talks, one-on-one mentoring, group sessions, and guidance on educational pathways. The initiative particularly focuses on supporting students in underserved or hard-to-reach communities, where access to mentorship and career guidance is often limited. By bringing alumni back into schools, the program creates a bridge between aspiration and opportunity, helping students better understand their future possibilities. Why This Opportunity Matters Many students in Ghana, particularly in underserved communities, lack access to relatable mentors, accurate career information, and practical guidance on life after school. This often leads to uncertainty and missed opportunities. Back to Inspire addresses this challenge by connecting students with alumni who have walked the same path. By sharing lived experiences, mentors can: Demystify career and education pathways Build students’ confidence and aspirations Strengthen school-level mentoring systems Support smoother transitions into higher education, employment, or entrepreneurship This initiative is not just about mentorship—it is about creating a ripple effect of knowledge, inspiration, and opportunity across generations . What You’ll Gain While this is a volunteer-based initiative, the benefits go far beyond financial rewards. Participants will gain: Leadership and Mentorship Experience – Strengthen your ability to guide, coach, and inspire young people Personal Fulfillment – Make a tangible difference in students’ lives and contribute to national development Professional Growth – Enhance your communication, leadership, and facilitation skills Expanded Network – Connect with fellow alumni, educators, and national education stakeholders Recognition and Impact – Be part of a nationally recognized initiative shaping the future of education in Ghana Additionally, selected alumni will receive training, safeguarding guidance, and facilitation support , ensuring they are well-prepared to engage effectively with students. Eligibility This opportunity is open to: Mastercard Foundation alumni in Ghana Individuals willing to return to their former SHS/SHTS or support schools in their region Alumni interested in mentoring and engaging with students Those passionate about youth development, education, and career guidance Individuals ready to contribute to a collaborative, national initiative No prior mentoring experience is required—just a willingness to give back and inspire. Application Process and Deadline Interested alumni are required to express their interest by completing a short online form . This initial step helps organizers: Understand alumni backgrounds and school affiliations Identify available resources and mentorship gaps Plan coordinated engagement with schools After submission: Alumni data will inform school selection and rollout planning Selected participants will receive onboarding and guidance Mentorship engagements will begin after final selections Before applying, read the official brief . After, you can apply via the official interest form. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Back to Inspire: Mastercard Foundation Ghana Alumni Mentorship Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AI Science Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship at Boston Consulting Group: A High-Impact Opportunity in Applied AI Research
summary: Introduction: Advancing Science Through AI Innovation In today’s rapidly evolving technological landscape, artificial intelligence is transforming how industries solve complex challenges—from healthcare breakthroughs to climate solutions. The AI Science Institute (AISI) Postdoctoral Fellowship by Boston Consulting Group represents a unique opportunity for early-career scientists to operate at the cutting edge of applied AI research while working on real-world, high-impact problems. Delivered through BCG X , this fellowship blends scientific exploration, industry application, and strategic impact, creating a rare bridge between academia and real-world deployment. About Boston Consulting Group and BCG X Founded in 1963, Boston Consulting Group is one of the world’s leading management consulting firms, known for driving transformation across industries. With a strong focus on integrating digital and human capabilities , BCG supports organizations in achieving sustainable growth and innovation. BCG X , the firm’s technology and innovation arm, brings together over 3,000 experts in AI, engineering, and design to solve some of the world’s most complex challenges. Through its global network, BCG X enables talent to build groundbreaking solutions that impact millions of lives. About the AI Science Institute Fellowship The AI Science Institute (AISI) Fellowship is an elite postdoctoral program designed to empower the next generation of applied science leaders. Unlike traditional academic or big-tech postdoctoral roles, this program places fellows at the intersection of: Advanced scientific research Real-world implementation Strategic decision-making Fellows will work on problems where data, models, and problem definitions are not fully established , requiring creativity, innovation, and interdisciplinary collaboration. Key Responsibilities and Learning Opportunities Participants in the AISI Fellowship will: 1. Conduct High-Impact Research Apply machine learning, simulation, optimization, and Generative AI to real-world scientific problems Lead or contribute to multidisciplinary research initiatives 2. Collaborate Across Disciplines Work closely with scientists, engineers, and business leaders Engage with global partners across industries 3. Publish and Innovate Author research papers in top-tier journals Co-develop intellectual property and innovative solutions 4. Build Scalable Solutions Design workflows that translate AI models into real-world tools Ensure reproducibility and scalability of research 5. Act as a Scientific Ambassador Represent AISI across projects Contribute to shaping research direction and impact Research Areas and Focus Themes Fellows will contribute to cutting-edge domains such as: AI-accelerated materials discovery Scientific foundation models (biology, chemistry, climate science) Simulation-augmented learning (physics-informed AI, reinforcement learning) Compute technologies (chip design, algorithm optimization) Quantum computing and hybrid workflows These areas reflect the program’s ambition to solve globally significant scientific and technological challenges . Program Structure and Benefits Duration 24 months, with the possibility of transitioning into a full-time role at BCG X Mentorship Dedicated mentorship from senior advisors and scientific leaders Resources Access to BCG’s global infrastructure Advanced computing resources Exclusive datasets and research ecosystems Compensation First-year base salary: $158,400 Additional support for: Publishing Conference participation Intellectual property development Comprehensive Benefits Package The fellowship offers a highly competitive benefits package, including: Full medical, dental, and vision coverage (no premiums for employees and families) Low-cost healthcare access Gym and fitness reimbursements Retirement contributions and profit-sharing Paid parental leave and family support programs Generous paid time off and holidays This ensures fellows can focus fully on research and innovation while maintaining personal well-being. Work Locations and Environment The program is based in major U.S. cities, including: Boston New York San Francisco Chicago Seattle Washington, DC Fellows are expected to: Work in-office as required Travel occasionally to client sites Engage in a fast-paced, high-impact work environment Why This Fellowship Stands Out This program is distinct because it: Bridges the gap between academia and industry Focuses on real-world deployment of AI solutions Encourages fellows to define and shape research problems Offers direct collaboration with global industry leaders It is ideal for those who want to move beyond theoretical research and create tangible, scalable impact . Application Process To apply, candidates must: Submit a Resume/CV Include a 1–2 page statement of interest outlining: Research background Areas of interest Motivation for joining AISI Provide links to publications, code, or relevant projects For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post AI Science Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship at Boston Consulting Group: A High-Impact Opportunity in Applied AI Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
